2024-10-24.title: She Embodied Japan’s #MeToo. With a Searing Film, She’s Ready to Move On.
2024-10-24.abstract: “Black Box Diaries,” a documentary about Shiori Ito’s fight against Japan’s patriarchal justice system, opens on Friday in the United States.

2024-10-24.title: Is Afghanistan’s Most-Wanted Militant Now Its Best Hope for Change?
2024-10-24.abstract: Sirajuddin Haqqani has tried to remake himself from blood-soaked jihadist to pragmatic Taliban statesman. Western diplomats are shocked — and enticed.

2024-10-24.title: World Opens to the Taliban Despite Their Shredding of Women’s Rights
2024-10-24.abstract: Taliban officials have scored a series of diplomatic victories this year that have started a subtle shift toward normalizing their government.

2024-10-24.title: What We Learned Talking to the Taliban’s Most Fearsome Leader
2024-10-24.abstract: Sirajuddin Haqqani, who has a $10 million American bounty on his head, is now positioning himself as a figure of relative moderation.

2024-10-25.title: In a Region of Majestic Beauty, Sunnis and Shiites Wage Bloody War
2024-10-25.abstract: In Kurram, near the Afghan border, Pakistan has been helpless to stop the latest outbreaks of a sectarian conflict that goes back decades.

2024-10-25.title: Israeli Strike Kills 3 Journalists in Southern Lebanon
2024-10-25.abstract: The three worked for Lebanese news organizations, their employers said. Lebanon’s health ministry said they had been staying in a residence housing journalists.

2024-10-25.title: A Pivotal Election for Georgia, Explained
2024-10-25.abstract: A parliamentary vote could decide whether the Caucasus nation will be oriented to the West or East for years to come. Here’s what to know.

2024-10-25.title: The Other War for These Gazans Is Against Cancer
2024-10-25.abstract: The small number of cancer patients from Gaza who are receiving care in Jordan’s capital, Amman, bear burdens beyond those of illness — deep feelings of guilt, along with fear and homesickness.

2024-10-25.title: Gay People Are Hunted Down and Beaten in a Country Once a Refuge
2024-10-25.abstract: Ivory Coast, long considered one of West Africa’s most tolerant societies, has recently seen a string of assaults on L.G.B.T.Q. people after several social media influencers exhorted their followers to “hunt” gay men.

2024-10-26.title: Why Did Israel Attack Iran?
2024-10-26.abstract: The two countries have been fighting a shadow war for years. Now, their conflict has burst into the open.

2024-10-26.title: Who Is Tommy Robinson, the Activist Behind a Far-Right London Rally?
2024-10-26.abstract: The founder of the English Defence League, whose real name is Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, is at the forefront of a growing movement that was behind anti-immigrant riots this summer.

2024-10-26.title: The Man Who Shaped China’s Strongman Rule Has a New Job: Winning Taiwan
2024-10-26.abstract: Xi Jinping’s top adviser, Wang Huning, is credited with shaping the authoritarianism that steered China’s rise. But can he influence Taiwan?

2024-10-26.title: Governing Party in Georgia Wins Election, as Opposition Calls for Protests
2024-10-26.abstract: The Georgian Dream party won 54 percent of the vote, the electoral commission said Sunday, a result that critics of the government fear could derail the country’s pro-Western course.

2024-10-26.title: Vatican Punts Question of Female Deacons at Major Meeting
2024-10-26.abstract: A meeting four years in the making said the ordination of women required further study, but it called for women to have more leadership roles, even in seminaries.

2024-10-27.title: Japan’s Long-Governing Party Loses Its Majority
2024-10-27.abstract: The Japanese electorate appeared to punish the Liberal Democrats, leaving it unclear whether the party would be forced to expand its coalition to stay in power.

2024-10-27.title: Ukraine Invaded Russia. Here’s What It Was Like for Civilians.
2024-10-27.abstract: Russians in the area of Ukraine’s invasion have described seeing signs of violent encounters, as well as respectful treatment from Ukrainian troops.

2024-10-27.title: How Years of Government Failures Caused a Flood ‘Worse Than Boko Haram’
2024-10-27.abstract: After a recent dam disaster, Nigerian officials blamed God, climate change and poor people. But experts had warned the dam was at risk well before it collapsed. The officials did nothing.

2024-10-27.title: Living on a Volcano’s Edge, Italians Practice for Disaster
2024-10-27.abstract: The Italian authorities carried out a drill of their plan to save a half-million people from toxic fumes, deadly quakes or a full eruption. Vesuvius isn’t the volcano they’re worried about.

2024-10-27.title: For Catholic Jubilee, Days of Joy and Sadness in Rome and Bethlehem
2024-10-27.abstract: For Rome, the rare Christmas Eve rite of redemption is cause for celebration this year. For Bethlehem, it is another grim reminder of the generations of war and deprivation.

2024-10-28.title: In England’s Most Haunted Village, Halloween Means Screams and Skeptics
2024-10-28.abstract: Pluckley is said to count at least 12 spirits among its 1,000 residents. Come October, ghost hunters arrive in droves to a place where even nonbelievers concede they’ve had eerie encounters.

2024-10-28.title: Bullied by China at Sea, With the Broken Bones to Prove It
2024-10-28.abstract: A violent attack on a Vietnamese fishing boat tests Hanoi’s muted but resolute approach to China’s aggression in the South China Sea.

2024-10-28.title: Israel Calls the Shots in the Mideast as U.S. Plays a Lesser Role
2024-10-28.abstract: A once pivotal player has seen its influence become limited.

2024-10-28.title: Uruguay’s Presidential Election Is Headed to a November Runoff
2024-10-28.abstract: Yamandú Orsi, a center-left former mayor, finished first on Sunday but fell short of the 50 percent threshold needed to avoid another round of voting.

2024-10-28.title: Ukrainian Civilians in Once Safe City Fear Growing Menace of Glide Bombs
2024-10-28.abstract: Cities in eastern and southern Ukraine are increasingly being hit by Russian glide bombs, converted gravity bombs that can be guided to targets, often landing without detection, residents and officials say.

2024-10-29.title: Arrests and Spying Charges Alarm Diplomats and Aid Workers in Yemen
2024-10-29.abstract: The Iran-backed Houthi militia has detained dozens of Yemenis linked to the U.S. Embassy or international organizations recently.

2024-10-29.title: Part-Time Farmers, Part-Time Rock Stars: A Chinese Band’s Unlikely Rise
2024-10-29.abstract: The band, Varihnaz, has gained fans by offering an alternative to China’s hyper-polished, fast-paced modern life, with songs about pesticides and poultry raising.

2024-10-29.title: The New Threat to Brazil’s Forests: Chemicals
2024-10-29.abstract: Authorities use satellites to track deforestation caused by chain saws or fire. Now criminals are using chemicals to degrade trees — and evade detection.

2024-10-29.title: After Shaky Start for Labour, First Budget Brings Chance for Reset
2024-10-29.abstract: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government has stumbled since his party won a landslide victory, but the financial plan to be announced on Wednesday offers a possible relaunch.

2024-10-29.title: With Limited Options, Zelensky Seeks a Path Forward for Ukraine
2024-10-29.abstract: A muted response to Ukraine’s “victory plan” and steep challenges on the battlefield leave Kyiv searching for a Plan B.

2024-10-30.title: Satellite Images Suggest Israel Struck a Major Iranian Missile Plant
2024-10-30.abstract: Analysts said the Shahroud Space Center was likely used to produce intermediate-range ballistic missiles that could be used to target Israel.

2024-10-30.title: China’s Latest Security Target: Halloween Partygoers
2024-10-30.abstract: Last year, the Shanghai government said Halloween celebrations were a sign of “cultural tolerance.” This year, the police rounded up people in costume.

2024-10-30.title: Was Stone Age Scandinavia Struck by Plague?
2024-10-30.abstract: New research by geneticists hints at the deadly work of Yersinia pestis 5,000 years ago.

2024-10-30.title: At a Glitzy Saudi Investment Forum, Almost No Mention of War
2024-10-30.abstract: Saudi Arabia and its Gulf neighbors are seeking to reassure foreign investors that it’s still safe to do business amid the turmoil around the Mideast.

2024-10-30.title: Flash Floods in Spain Leave at Least 95 Dead
2024-10-30.abstract: About 1,000 soldiers from emergency response units deployed to the affected areas, and the death toll was expected to rise after one of the worst natural disasters to hit the country in recent years.

2024-10-31.title: 8 Supreme Court Justices in Mexico to Resign Ahead of Contentious Election
2024-10-31.abstract: All but three of the country’s Supreme Court justices announced they would quit rather than partake in the controversial elections mandated by a judicial overhaul.

2024-10-31.title: Mexico Passes Bill Barring Legal Challenges to Constitutional Changes
2024-10-31.abstract: The bill has drawn criticism from legal scholars who say it would bulldoze any judicial oversight of constitutional matters.

2024-10-31.title: North Korea, in the Spotlight Over Ukraine, Launches a Long-Range Missile
2024-10-31.abstract: The launch, into waters west of Japan, came shortly after the United States and South Korea criticized the North for sending troops to join Russia’s war.

2024-10-31.title: What Sank the Tech Tycoon’s ‘Unsinkable’ Yacht?
2024-10-31.abstract: A Times investigation has found that an unusually tall mast, and the design changes it required, made a superyacht owned by a British tech mogul vulnerable to capsizing.

2024-10-31.title: How the U.S. Election Matters for the Rest of the World
2024-10-31.abstract: Times foreign correspondents describe what’s at stake for the world, including China, Israel, Ukraine, global trade and climate.
